title: The Patent System: Purpose, Rationale & Global Architecture
subtitle: Why Patents Exist and How the System Works
summary: A rigorous first-principles examination of why patent systems exist, the quid-pro-quo theory of disclosure, and how the major national and international frameworks relate. Traces the evolution from the Statute of Monopolies to the modern TRIPS-WTO architecture.

figures.caption: Global patent filing volumes by jurisdiction (2022–present)
figures.changeNote: WIPO 2024 data shows 3.5M global applications vs 3.2M in print edition.

title: Patentability: Novelty, Inventive Step & Industrial Applicability
subtitle: The Three Pillars of a Valid Patent
summary: A deep-dive into patentability criteria under Indian, US and European law. Examines absolute vs relative novelty, the person-skilled-in-the-art standard, and how inventive step is assessed with worked examples from real prosecution histories.
keyTopics: TSM test and Graham v. John Deere

figures.caption: Novelty assessment framework: India vs USPTO vs EPO

title: Subject-Matter Eligibility: What Can (and Cannot) Be Patented
subtitle: Navigating Section 3, Alice/Mayo, and EPO Exclusions
summary: Maps the excluded subject-matter landscape - Section 3(d), 3(k) and 3(m) under Indian law, Alice-Mayo under US law, and the EPO technical-character requirement. Includes practical claim-drafting strategies to overcome eligibility rejections.
keyTopics: Claim drafting strategies for software inventions

title: The Patent Document: Structure, Anatomy & Formal Requirements
subtitle: Understanding Every Section of a Patent Application
summary: Comprehensive walkthrough of every section of a patent application - title, field, background, summary, detailed description, claims, abstract - with annotated examples drawn from real Indian and US patents.

title: Claims Architecture: The Claim Set as a Fortress
subtitle: Designing a Multi-Layered Claim Portfolio
summary: The pivot of the book. Introduces the fortress metaphor for claim architecture - independent claims as outer walls, dependent claims as fallback positions, method/system/CRM triplets, omnibus claims, and claim-set stress testing.
keyTopics: Jepson claims and open/closed transitional phrases

title: Drafting Methodology: From Invention Disclosure to Filing-Ready Application
subtitle: A Practitioner's Step-by-Step Process
summary: A systematic drafting methodology - from the initial invention disclosure meeting through claim mapping, specification writing, and internal review - with checklists and templates practitioners can adopt immediately.

title: Specification Writing: Description, Drawings & Enablement
subtitle: Writing Specifications That Hold Up in Prosecution and Litigation
summary: Deep-dives into specification writing - using drawings effectively, creating an enabling disclosure, avoiding written-description pitfalls, and building support for the broadest possible claims across all embodiments.

title: Examiner Psychology & the Art of Anticipation
subtitle: Drafting to Survive the Examination Gauntlet
summary: A candid look at how patent examiners work - their incentive structures, search patterns, art-unit biases, and common rejection strategies - and how to draft claims that are both broad and examination-resilient.
keyTopics: Examiner incentive structures and art-unit culture

title: Patent Prosecution: India IPO - Procedure, Timelines & Strategy
subtitle: Navigating the Indian Patent Office End-to-End
summary: Complete guide to Indian patent prosecution - filing, publication, examination request, examination report, hearings, grant, and post-grant opposition. Includes current official fees and strategic timelines.
keyTopics: India filing procedures and official forms

figures.caption: India IPO official fees - filing, examination, grant (as of print)
figures.changeNote: Indian IPO fee revision effective April 2026 - see current fee schedule on the Fees page.

title: USPTO Prosecution: Office Actions, Appeals & Allowance Strategy
subtitle: Mastering the US Patent Prosecution Lifecycle
summary: End-to-end USPTO prosecution - from filing to allowance or appeal. Covers responding to §102/§103/§112 rejections, the AFCP 2.0 program, after-final practice, the PTAB appeal process, and continuation strategy.

figures.caption: USPTO official fees - filing, search, examination (FY2026)
figures.changeNote: USPTO fee adjustment effective Jan 2026 - see current fee schedule on the Fees page.

title: PCT Practice: International Filing, the ISR & National Phase Entry
subtitle: Using the PCT System Strategically
summary: Comprehensive PCT guide - Chapter I and Chapter II procedures, choosing receiving offices, international search and preliminary examination, optimizing the national phase entry decision, and using PCT delays strategically.
keyTopics: PCT Chapter I and Chapter II
International Search Report and Written Opinion
National phase entry decisions and timing

figures.caption: PCT official fees - transmittal, international search, designation (2026)
figures.changeNote: PCT fees updated as of January 2026 - see current fee schedule.

title: EPO Practice: European Patent Applications & EPC Prosecution
subtitle: Filing and Prosecuting Before the European Patent Office
summary: Guide to EPO prosecution under the EPC - filing strategy, the search and examination phases, opposition proceedings, and the validation country decision. Covers the Unitary Patent and Unified Patent Court.

figures.caption: EPO official fees - filing, search, claims, grant (2026)

title: Responding to Examination: Claim Amendments, Arguments & Interviews
subtitle: The Craft of the Office Action Response
summary: Structured framework for responding to office actions across all jurisdictions - when to amend vs argue, claim differentiation strategies, the art of the examiner interview, and building prosecution history with appeal in mind.

title: AI in Patent Practice: Tools, Capabilities & Limitations
subtitle: A Practitioner's Honest Assessment of AI for IP Work
summary: A rigorous, practitioner-focused survey of AI tools in the IP landscape - prior-art search engines, claim drafting assistants, prosecution analytics, and LLM-based drafting. Covers capabilities, limitations, and ethical obligations.
keyTopics: AI prior-art search tools (Google Patents AI, Lens.org, Patsnap)
Ethical obligations and unauthorized practice risks

title: The AI Prompt Library: Frameworks for IP Work
subtitle: Tested Prompts for Every Stage of the Patent Lifecycle
summary: The core of the AI companion. Provides the full structured prompt library with 60+ tested prompts covering drafting, prosecution, prior-art research, claims analysis, FTO, and strategy - with usage notes and worked examples.

title: Patent Portfolio Strategy: Building, Managing & Monetising IP
subtitle: From Single Patent to Strategic Portfolio
summary: Strategic framework for building and managing a patent portfolio - landscape analysis, white-space identification, portfolio pruning, licensing strategy, patent assertion, and the build-buy-license decision framework.

title: India's Innovation Paradox: The IP Gap and the Path Forward
subtitle: Why India's Patent Filing Lags Its Innovation Output
summary: The book's policy chapter. Examines the statistical gap between India's startup growth and its patent filings, the structural causes, global comparators, and a framework for what practitioners, policymakers, and institutions can do.
keyTopics: India startup vs patent filing statistics
The practitioner's role in building IP culture

figures.caption: India patent applications per 1000 startups vs China, US, South Korea (2022–2024)
figures.changeNote: Updated with 2024 WIPO data. India's ratio improved marginally but gap persists.

title: IP Due Diligence, Valuation & Transactions
subtitle: Patents in M&A, Investment, and Licensing Transactions
summary: Practical guide to IP due diligence - for M&A, investment rounds, and licensing deals. Covers patent valuation methodologies, freedom-to-operate analyses, IP representations and warranties, and deal structuring.
keyTopics: IP due diligence checklist for M&A

title: The Future of Patents: AI Inventorship, Emerging Technologies & Global Trends
subtitle: Where Patent Law is Heading
summary: Forward-looking capstone chapter - AI inventorship debates (DABUS and beyond), patents for quantum computing, biotechnology, and advanced materials, the evolving role of the patent agent in an AI-augmented world.
keyTopics: AI inventorship: DABUS and legal developments
Quantum computing and biotech patent trends
The future role of the patent practitioner

figures.caption: AI-related patent applications by jurisdiction (2020–2024)
figures.changeNote: Rapid growth in AI-related filings - updated figures from WIPO 2024 Technology Trends.
